instruction: Classify the meter/grid event description into an event type.
baseline_prompt: You are a smart-grid operations assistant. Classify the meter/grid event description into an event type. Classify into one of these categories: outage, overconsumption, tamper, voltage_anomaly, billing_dispute, maintenance.
source: Authored for TEI-Bench (Sonnet-generated, Sonnet-validated; kept only label-agreed examples).

train.query: Storm damage caused complete service disruption in coastal zone. Smart meters report blackout conditions starting 16:52. Crews dispatched to restore supply.

Suburban household at 847 Maple Avenue showing real-time demand of 22 kW sustained over two hours, well beyond normal peak loads and potentially indicating malfunctioning equipment or unusual appliance operation.
Remote disconnect command failed, physical inspection shows meter cover removed and reconnection performed manually

Monitoring data reveals that dwelling unit 23C experienced a rapid rise to 253V at 22:47 last night, triggering overvoltage warnings on sensitive electronics. Duration was brief at approximately 3 seconds.
Agricultural account holder contests irrigation season charges, stating the calculated water pump electricity costs don't align with their recorded operational hours and pump specifications.
SCADA communication equipment at remote site Delta-5 needs firmware upgrade to patch security vulnerabilities. Scheduling 4-hour service window.
Industrial facility meter shows flat zero consumption for past 3 hours starting midnight. Plant supervisor confirms production halted due to complete power failure.
Warehouse facility meter reading indicates 340 kWh consumed between midnight and 6 AM, dramatically elevated from typical overnight baseline of 45-60 kWh for climate control only.
Customer usage pattern suddenly flatlined after years of normal variation, meter case shows tool marks and unauthorized opening

Single-phase meter at commercial bakery shows morning readings consistently elevated to 245-249V range during low-demand periods, normalizing to 232V only when neighboring businesses commence operations.
Consumer filed formal complaint that estimated charges during meter replacement period were calculated at 3x their historical average consumption without justification.
Pole-mounted disconnect switch at location 88-NE has corroded hardware. Field crew to replace mounting brackets and apply anti-corrosion treatment.
Regional blackout affecting grid sectors A-14 through A-19. All meters in affected zones reporting loss of supply since 20:45. Backup systems engaged.
Commercial facility on Pine Street drew 1,200 kW continuously for six hours yesterday, representing a dramatic increase from their usual 300-400 kW range during similar operational periods.
Voltage present but negligible energy registration, internal shunt resistor values inconsistent with factory specifications

test.query: Residential meter 4782-B registered a sustained drop to 198V at 14:23 hours, remaining below the 220V nominal specification for approximately 47 seconds before self-correction. Customer appliances may have experienced performance degradation during this period.
Account representative forwarded case where customer's January statement reflects warm-weather consumption levels despite freezing temperatures and documented furnace usage.
Routine vegetation management along transmission corridor from mile marker 14 to 28 needed before storm season. Tree trimming crews to be deployed.
Apartment complex lost all electricity at 13:07 following loud transformer noise. Meter communications still active but showing no energy delivery to units.
Residential account 9012 recorded 89 kWh consumption in a single hour last night, far exceeding the monthly peak demand we've seen from this address over the past two years.
Reverse energy flow recorded during peak consumption hours at residential address, wiring configuration appears altered
Instantaneous measurement at meter 5594 captured a sudden depression to 189V coinciding with nearby motor startup at manufacturing facility. Recovery took 1.2 seconds to return to 227V baseline.

Customer called stating their latest invoice shows 450 kWh usage for a vacation home that was completely unoccupied during the entire billing cycle, requesting immediate review and adjustment of charges.
Capacitor bank at industrial park substation making audible humming noise. Visual examination and capacitance testing recommended to assess condition.
Customer reports all lights went out suddenly at 22:14. Smart meter data shows complete cessation of power flow. No energy consumption recorded since incident time.
Apartment 5C at Riverside Complex consumed 18 kWh in three hours this morning, approximately five times their historical morning usage and triple the building average per unit.
Meter reports zero consumption for 72 hours while customer clearly occupying premises, bypass wiring suspected behind device

Smart meter alert from industrial complex on Riverside Drive indicates three instances of supply dropping to 201V between 06:00 and 06:15, each lasting 8-12 seconds. Pattern suggests upstream distribution issue.
Customer insists the $1,200 invoice is incorrect because they installed solar panels two months ago and expected to see credits, not increased charges on their account.
Transformer T-447 requires oil level check and bushing inspection scheduled for next Tuesday at 0600 hours before peak demand begins.
Farmhouse at rural meter ID M-99421 experiencing total power loss as of 06:35. Remote diagnostics confirm no current flowing through service lines.
Agricultural account pumping station recorded 2,100 kWh over 8-hour period, representing abnormal electricity draw that's inconsistent with seasonal irrigation patterns and equipment specifications.
Meter seal broken on unit 4782, housing shows signs of unauthorized access, internal components disturbed
Grid sensor array detected coordinated depression across Zone 7 meters, with levels falling to 203-208V for approximately 25 seconds at 11:34 AM, likely due to capacitor bank switching event.
Tenant disputes final move-out charges claiming they only occupied the apartment for 12 days of the billing period but were charged for the full month at residential rates.
Preventive inspection of underground cable joints in neighborhood distribution network shows moisture ingress in vault V-223. Resealing work required.
All three phases down in sector 7B since 03:15 this morning. Approximately 850 customers reporting no electricity. Transformer station TR-442 appears offline.
Multi-tenant building experiencing aggregate demand of 450 kWh during morning hours, substantially higher than the 120-150 kWh range observed during comparable periods last month.
Consumption readings frozen at same value for 11 consecutive days while neighbor meters show typical daily patterns, device integrity compromised
